>.purpose: Purpose:  >    at the end of a name identifies the name as a vector. >>   at the end of a name identifies the name as a dyadic. >>>  at the end of a name identifies the name as a triadic.
syntax: Syntax 1:  nameA>    is treated as a vector.
Syntax 2:  nameB>>   is treated as a dyadic.
Syntax 3:  nameC>>   is treated as a triadic or higher-order polyadic.

0>.purpose: Purpose:  0>    is the zero vector  (0 magnitude and a direction). 0>>   is the zero dyadic  (0 magnitude and two directions). 0>>>  is the zero triadic (0 magnitude and three directions).
syntax: Syntax 1:  0>            Note:  0>    is a vector, NOT equal to scalar 0.

1>>.purpose: Purpose:  1>>  is the unit dyadic whose defining property is Dot( 1>>, anyVector> ) = anyVector>

!.purpose: Purpose:  Opens an operating system command shell or issues an operating system command.

%.purpose: Purpose:  Marks the beginning of a comment. Marks the beginning of a comment that is to be incorporated in a C, Fortran, or MATLAB programs.
syntax: Syntax 2:  %% Put a comment to be written to code here.

&.purpose: Purpose:  The ampersand  &  is the multi-line continuation symbol. It instructs the program to read the next line in a file. & is not useful for keyboard input.

+.purpose: Purpose:  Adds or subtracts scalars, matrices, vectors, dyadics, etc. +  Declares a real scalar as inherently non-negative. -  Declares a real scalar as inherently non-positive.
syntax: Syntax 1:  a + b   returns the sum of a and b.

*.purpose: Purpose:  Multiplies scalars, matrices, vectors, dyadics, etc., or divides these quantities by a scalar.
syntax: Syntax 1:  a * b   returns a multiplied by b.
Syntax 2:  a / s   returns a divided by the scalar s.
Syntax:  a ^ b    returns a raised to the power b.

abs.title: abs
abs.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the absolute value of a real scalar expression or the modulus of a complex expression.

acos.title: acos
acos.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the inverse cosine of a scalar expression.

asin.title: asin
asin.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the inverse sine of a scalar expression.

atan.title: atan or atan2
atan.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the inverse tangent of a scalar expression or the the numerical value of the two-argument inverse tangent.

AddForce.title: AddForce
AddForce.purpose: Purpose:  Adds (or resets) force on a point or particle.
syntax: Syntax 1 is frequently used for external forces such as Earth's
Syntax 2 is frequently used for translational springs/dampers,

AddForceDamper.title: AddForceDamper
AddForceDamper.purpose: Purpose:  Applies damper forces between two points.

AddForceElectrostatic.title: AddForceElectrostatic
AddForceElectrostatic.purpose: Purpose:  Applies an inverse-square electrostatic force on two points.

AddForceGravity.title: AddForceGravity
AddForceGravity.purpose: Purpose:  Applies a force associated with the resultant force produced by a uniform parallel gravitational field, to the mass center of a massive object (e.g., a Particle or RigidBody) or to all particles and/or mass centers of all rigid bodies in a System.

AddForceInverseSquare.title: AddForceInverseSquare
AddForceInverseSquare.purpose: Purpose:  Applies an inverse-square force on a point from another point.

AddForceLinearActuator.title: AddForceLinearActuator
AddForceLinearActuator.purpose: Purpose:  Applies a linear actuator force between two points.

AddForceSpring.title: AddForceSpring
AddForceSpring.purpose: Purpose:  Applies spring forces between two points.
syntax: Syntax 2:  Q.AddForceSpring( P, k, Ln, stretchExponent )

AddTorque.title: AddTorque
AddTorque.purpose: Purpose:  Adds (or resets) torque to a RigidFrame or RigidBody.

AddTorqueDamper.title: AddTorqueDamper
AddTorqueDamper.purpose: Purpose:  Adds damper torque to a RigidFrame or RigidBody.

Animate.title: Animate
Animate.purpose: Purpose:  Creates output for objects to be animated by a computer graphic visualization program, e.g., MGView (Web) or ANIMAKE (Windows/PC).

ceil.title: ceil
ceil.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the ceiling or floor of a real scalar expression.

Clear.title: Clear
Clear.purpose: Purpose:  Clears the workspace by restarting the program. Re-runs designated lines from the workspace.

Constant.title: Constant
Constant.purpose: Purpose:  Declares the names of constants.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Constant        a,  b,  c+,  d-

cos.title: cos and sec
cos.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the cosine or secant of a scalar expression.

cosh.title: cosh,
cosh.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the hyperbolic cosine, sine, or tangent.

Cross.title: Cross
Cross.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the cross-product or dot-product for vectors, dyadics, etc.

Derivative.title: Derivative
Derivative.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the ordinary or partial derivative of a scalar, vector, or dyadic expression or a matrix of such expressions.

Dt.title: Dt
Dt.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the ordinary derivative of a scalar, vector, or dyadic expression or a matrix of such expressions.

D.title: D
D.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the partial derivative of a scalar, vector, or dyadic expression or a matrix of such expressions.

GetJacobian.title: GetJacobian
GetJacobian.purpose: Purpose:  Forms a Jacobian whose elements are partial derivatives, e.g., partial derivatives of velocity with respect to generalized speeds.

GetPartialVelocity.title: GetPartials
GetPartialVelocity.purpose: Purpose:  Forms partial velocities and partial angular velocities.

Dyadic.title: Dyadic
syntax: Syntax 1:  Dyadic( A,  xx, yy, zz )
Syntax 2:  Dyadic( A,  xx,xy,xz, yx,yy,yz, zx,zy,zz )

Echo.title: Echo
Echo.purpose: Purpose:  Display messages on the screen.

Encode.title: ConstantEncode
Encode.purpose: Purpose:  Instructs MATLAB(R), C, and Fortran programs to incorporate additional inputs, outputs, or calculations that may not otherwise appear because of code-optimization.
syntax: Syntax 1:  ConstantEncode  -- type  HELP CONSTANT   for information.
Syntax 2:  SpecifiedEncode -- type  HELP SPECIFIED  for information.
Syntax 3:  OutputEncode    -- type  HELP OUTPUT     for information.

Epsilon.title: Epsilon
Epsilon.purpose: Purpose:  Rounds numbers that are suitably close to an integer.

Euler.title: Euler -- one of the greatest engineer / mathematician of all time.

Gibbs.title: Gibbs -- one of the greatest engineer / mathematician of all time.

Evaluate.title: Evaluate,
Evaluate.purpose: Purpose:  Evaluates an expression by replacing designated symbols.

exp.title: exp
exp.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the exponential function of a scalar expression.

Expand.title: Expand or ExpandDeep
Expand.purpose: Purpose:  Produces an expression that is mathematically equivalent to the given expression but usually containing fewer parentheses.

Explicit.title: Explicit
Explicit.purpose: Purpose:  Substitutes the right-hand-side of all names in an expressions, repeating this process until no names that have a right-hand-side remain the expression.

Express.title: Express
Express.purpose: Purpose:  Expresses a vector, dyadic, or other tensor in terms of basis vectors fixed in RigidFrame or RigidBody.

Factor.title: Factor
Factor.purpose: Purpose:  Useful to simplify results or compare results with another analyst. Rearranges expression by regrouping on certain factors.

FactorLinear.title: FactorLinear
FactorLinear.purpose: Purpose:  Useful to simplify results or compare results with another analyst. Rearranges expression by regrouping terms in designated names. FactorLinear    rearranges terms in groups of degree 1. FactorQuadratic rearranges terms in groups of degree 1 and 2.

factorial.title: factorial
factorial.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the factorial of a non-negative integer.

Exclude.title: Exclude
Exclude.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the portion of an expression that remains when terms containing designated names are excluded.

Include.title: Include
Include.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the portion of an expression that contains designated names

GetAcceleration.title: GetAcceleration
GetAcceleration.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a point's acceleration in a reference frame or gets a point's acceleration relative to another point in a reference frame.

GetAngleBetweenVectors.title: GetAngleBetweenVectors
GetAngleBetweenVectors.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the angle between two vectors.

GetAngularAcceleration.title: GetAngularAcceleration
GetAngularAcceleration.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a rigid frame/body's angular acceleration in a reference frame.

GetAngularMomentum.title: GetAngularMomentum
GetAngularMomentum.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates an object's angular momentum about a point.

GetAngularSpeed.title: GetAngularSpeed
GetAngularSpeed.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a rigid frame/body's angular speed in a reference frame.

GetAngularVelocity.title: GetAngularVelocity
GetAngularVelocity.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a rigid frame/body's angular velocity in a reference frame.

GetBasisVector.title: GetBasisVector
GetBasisVector.purpose: Purpose:  Returns a unit vector that was created when a RigidBody RigidFrame, or NewtonianFrame declaration was issued

GetCharge.title: GetCharge
GetCharge.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the charge on an object.

GetCmAcceleration.title: GetCmAcceleration
GetCmAcceleration.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a system's center of mass acceleration in a reference frame.

GetCmPosition.title: GetCmPosition
GetCmPosition.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a system's center of mass position from another point.

GetCmVelocity.title: GetCmVelocity
GetCmVelocity.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a system's center of mass velocity in a reference frame.

GetCoefficient.title: GetCoefficient
GetCoefficient.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the coefficient of a name or names in an expression.

GetColumns.title: GetRows
GetColumns.purpose: Purpose:  Reports the number of rows or columns of a matrix. Creates a matrix from designated rows or columns of a matrix.
syntax: Syntax 3:     GetRows( mat,  i, j, k,  n:m,  ... )
Syntax 4:  GetColumns( mat,  i, j, k,  n:m,  ... )

GetDefaults.title: GetDefaults
syntax: Syntax 2:  SetDefaults( AUTOLEV )         // Type HELP AUTOLEV for more info.
Syntax 3:  SetDefaults( MotionGenesis )   // This is the default setting.

AUTOLEV.title: AUTOLEV
AUTOLEV.purpose: Purpose:  Sets defaults to be compatible with older versions of the program.
syntax: Syntax 2:  SetDefaults( MotionGenesis )   // This is the default setting.

GetCofactor.title: GetCofactor
GetCofactor.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates the minor or cofactor of a matrix.

GetDeterminant.title: GetDeterminant
GetDeterminant.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the determinant of a matrix.

GetDiagonalMatrix.title: GetDiagonalMatrix
GetDiagonalMatrix.purpose: Purpose:  Forms diagonal matrices. Forms a column matrix from diagonal elements of a matrix.

GetDistance.title: GetDistance
GetDistance.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the distance (or its square) between two points.

GetEffectiveForce.title: GetEffectiveForce
GetEffectiveForce.purpose: Purpose:  Gets effective force in the NewtonianFrame (or a designated frame).

GetEigen.title: GetEigen
GetEigen.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates the eigenvalues or the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a real square matrix or a dyadic whose measures are real numbers.

[].purpose: Purpose:  Assigns or returns one or more elements of a matrix. Use  GetElement  to return elements for an unnamed matrix.
syntax: Syntax 4:  mat2D[ iStart : iStop,  jStart : jStop ]

GetElement.title: GetElement
GetElement.purpose: Purpose:  Returns one or more elements from an unnamed matrix expression. Type  Help []  for simpler syntax for a named matrix.
syntax: Syntax 4:  GetElement( mat2DExpression,  iStart : iStop,  jStart : jStop )

GetElongation.title: GetElongation
GetElongation.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the time-derivative of the distance between two points.

GetEnergyCheckKane.title: GetEnergyCheckKane
GetEnergyCheckKane.purpose: Purpose:  Returns an energy checking function associated with Kane's dynamical equations, which theoretically remains constant throughout numerical integration of equations of motion.

GetEnvironmentValue.title: GetEnvironmentValue
GetEnvironmentValue.purpose: Purpose:  Displays the value of an operating system environment variable.

GetForceElectrostaticPotentialEnergy.title: GetForceElectrostaticPotentialEnergy
GetForceElectrostaticPotentialEnergy.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates potential energy associated with electrostatic forces.

GetForceInverseSquarePotentialEnergy.title: GetForceInverseSquarePotentialEnergy
GetForceInverseSquarePotentialEnergy.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates potential energy associated with an inverse-square force.

GetForceGravityPotentialEnergy.title: GetForceGravityPotentialEnergy
GetForceGravityPotentialEnergy.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates potential energy associated with gravity forces.

GetForceSpringPotentialEnergy.title: GetForceSpringPotentialEnergy
GetForceSpringPotentialEnergy.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates potential energy associated with a spring force.
syntax: Syntax 2:  Q.GetForceSpringPotentialEnergy( P, k, Ln, stretchExponent )

GetGeneralizedEffectiveForce.title: GetGeneralizedEffectiveForce
GetGeneralizedEffectiveForce.purpose: Purpose:  Forms expressions for generalized effective forces or contributions to generalized effective forces.

GetGeneralizedForce.title: GetGeneralizedForce
GetGeneralizedForce.purpose: Purpose:  Forms expressions for a system's generalized forces.

GetGeneralizedMomentum.title: GetGeneralizedMomentum
GetGeneralizedMomentum.purpose: Purpose:  Forms expressions for generalized momenta or contributions to generalized momenta.

GetIdentityMatrix.title: GetZeroMatrix
GetIdentityMatrix.purpose: Purpose:  Forms a square or rectangular zero or identity matrix.

GetInertiaDyadic.title: GetInertiaDyadic
GetInertiaDyadic.purpose: Purpose:  Gets an object's inertia dyadic about a point.

GetInertiaMatrix.title: GetInertiaMatrix
GetInertiaMatrix.purpose: Purpose:  Gets an object's inertia matrix about a point.

GetInertiaScalar.title: GetInertiaScalar
GetInertiaScalar.purpose: Purpose:  Gets an object's inertia scalar about a point for two unit vectors.

GetInertiaVector.title: GetInertiaVector
GetInertiaVector.purpose: Purpose:  Gets an object's inertia vector for a unit vector.

GetInverse.title: GetInverse
GetInverse.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the inverse of a matrix or a dyadic.

GetTranslationalMomentum.title: GetTranslationalMomentum
GetTranslationalMomentum.purpose: Purpose:  Gets translational (linear) momentum in a reference frame.

GetKineticEnergy.title: GetKineticEnergy
GetKineticEnergy.purpose: Purpose:  Gets kinetic energy in the NewtonianFrame (or a designated frame).

GetMagnitude.title: GetMagnitude
GetMagnitude.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates the magnitude of a vector or its magnitude squared.

GetMassUnit.title: GetMassUnit
GetMassUnit.purpose: Purpose:  Displays the mass, length, time, or angle unit. Stands for the unit of mass, length, or time set by the SetUnitSystem command.

GetMemory.title: GetMemory
GetMemory.purpose: Purpose:  Reports amount of dynamically allocated program memory.

GetMomentOfEffectiveForce.title: GetMomentOfEffectiveForce
GetMomentOfEffectiveForce.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates an object's moment of effective force about a point.

GetMomentOfForces.title: GetMomentOfForces
GetMomentOfForces.purpose: Purpose:  Sums the moments of all forces applied to an object.

GetMomentOfInertia.title: GetMomentOfInertia
GetMomentOfInertia.purpose: Purpose:  Gets an object's moment of inertia about a line.

GetPolynomialRoots.title: GetPolynomialRoots GetQuadraticRoots
GetPolynomialRoots.purpose: Purpose:  GetPolynomialRoots  gets the roots of a polynomial of degree Purpose:  GetPolynomial       creates a polynomial function from a matrix of coefficients or determines the coefficients of the leading terms of the Taylor-series expansion of a function about 0.
syntax: Syntax 1:  GetPolynomialRoots(       M  )       % M  is row or column matrix.
Syntax 3:  GetPolynomial(          M,  x )      % M  is row or column matrix.

GetEulerParameterMatrix44.title: GetEulerParameterMatrix44
GetEulerParameterMatrix44.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the 4x4 Euler parameter matrix that multiplies a 4x1 Euler- parameter matrix to produce the 4 Euler parameters associated with concatenating two rotation matrices.
syntax: Syntax:  GetEulerParameterMatrix44( e0, e1, e2, e3 )

GetPosition.title: GetPosition
GetPosition.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the position of a point from another point.

GetPotentialEnergy.title: GetPotentialEnergy
GetPotentialEnergy.purpose: Purpose:  Help with potential energy associated with various forces.

GetPower.title: GetPower
GetPower.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the power of all forces and torques in the system.

GetProductOfInertia.title: GetProductOfInertia
GetProductOfInertia.purpose: Purpose:  Gets an object's product of inertia about a point for two orthogonal unit vectors.

GetRadiusOfGyration.title: GetRadiusOfGyration
GetRadiusOfGyration.purpose: Purpose:  Gets an object's radius of gyration about a line.

GetResultantForce.title: GetResultantForce
GetResultantForce.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the sum of forces applied to an object.
syntax: Syntax 2:  Q.GetResultantForce( P )    or       Q.GetNetForce( P )

GetResultantTorque.title: GetResultantTorque
GetResultantTorque.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the sum of torques applied to a rigid object.
syntax: Syntax 2:  B.GetResultantTorque( A )    or       B.GetNetTorque( A )

GetRhs.title: GetRhs
GetRhs.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the expression that is assigned to a name. For example, if  y = 3*sin(t),  GetRhs(y)  returns 3*sin(t).

GetRotationMatrix.title: GetRotationMatrix
GetRotationMatrix.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the rotation matrix relating two sets of bases vectors.

GetRotationMatrixDyadic.title: GetRotationMatrixDyadic
GetRotationMatrixDyadic.purpose: Purpose:  Gets the rotation matrix dyadic relating two sets of bases vectors.

GetState.title: GetState
GetState.purpose: Purpose:  Creates an array of all names that are currently regarded as state.

GetSpeed.title: GetSpeed
GetSpeed.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a point's speed in a reference frame.

GetStatics.title: GetStatics
GetStatics.purpose: Purpose:  Gets equations governing static equilibrium.
syntax: Syntax 4:  System.GetStaticsLagrange()  IN DEVELOPMENT.  SEE SYNTAX.

SVD.title: SVD
SVD.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates the singular values (and possibly singular vectors) of a real matrix.

GetTaylorSeries.title: GetTaylorSeries
GetTaylorSeries.purpose: Purpose:  Expands functions in Taylor series. Use Linearize  for a Taylor series with the 0th and 1st terms. Use Linearize1 for a Taylor series with just the 1st term.
syntax: Syntax:  GetTaylorSeries( F, Range, arg1, arg2, ... )

Linearize.title: Linearize
Linearize.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the sum of the 0th and 1st term of a Taylor series or returns just the 0th or 1st terms of that Taylor series.
syntax: Syntax:  Linearize(  F, arg1, arg2, ... )  --  0th and 1st terms.

GetNorm.title: GetNorm
GetNorm.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the 2-norm of a real one-dimensional (row or column) matrix.

GetTrace.title: GetTrace
GetTrace.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the trace of a matrix or dyadic.

GetTranspose.title: GetTranspose
GetTranspose.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the transpose of a matrix or dyadic.

GetUnitVector.title: GetUnitVector
GetUnitVector.purpose: Purpose:  Forms a unit vector in the same direction as a given vector.

GetUnitSystem.title: GetUnitSystem
GetUnitSystem.purpose: Purpose:  Displays the dimensions of a quantity. Displays the names of all units recognized by the program.

GetVelocity.title: GetVelocity
GetVelocity.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a point's velocity in a reference frame or gets a point's velocity relative to another point in a reference frame.

GetVelocityRemainder.title: GetVelocityRemainder
GetVelocityRemainder.purpose: Purpose:  Forms velocity and angular velocity remainder.

if.title: if
if.purpose: Purpose:  Controls whether or not certain lines are executed in a script depending on whether a logical expression evaluates to true. If true, statements contained within braces are executed. Otherwise, the statements are skipped. Note: if statements only work in a script -- not when entering commands interactively at…

ImaginaryNumber.title: ImaginaryNumber
ImaginaryNumber.purpose: Purpose:  Declares the name assigned to the square root of -1. Returns  the name assigned to the square root of -1.

IndependentVariable.title: IndependentVariable
IndependentVariable.purpose: Purpose:  Declares the name assigned to the independent variable. Returns  the name assigned to the independent variable.

Input.title: Input
Input.purpose: Purpose:  Assigns/reports numerical values (with optional units) to constants, initial values, and numerical integration parameters for use by the ODE, Solve, EvaluateAtInput, and EvaluateToNumber commands.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Input a = v1 unitsA,  b = v2 unitsB,  c = v3,  d = v4 noUnits
Syntax 3:  Input( a )                or  Input( a + 2*b + 3*c )
Syntax 4:  InputNoConversion( a )    or  InputNoConversion( a + 2*b + 3*c )
Syntax 5:  InputWithConversion( a )  or  InputWithConversion( a + 2*b + 3*c )

int.title: int
int.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the integer portion of a real scalar expression.

Integrate.title: Integrate
Integrate.purpose: Purpose:  Integrates a scalar function or matrix of scalar functions.
syntax: Syntax:  Integrate( scalarOrMatrixExpression,  x = xi : xf )

IsPositive.title: IsNegative
IsPositive.purpose: Purpose:  Return a value that depends on the sign of a real scalar expression.
syntax: Syntax 1:  IsNegative( x )       % Returns  true  if x - 0,  otherwise  false.
Syntax 4:  IsPositive( x )       % Returns  true  if x >  0,  otherwise  false.

IsContainSymbol.title: IsContainSymbol
IsContainSymbol.purpose: Purpose:  Checks whether an expression contains a certain symbol.

IsSimplifyZero.title: IsSimplifyZero
IsSimplifyZero.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether a mathematical expression simplifies to zero (or within a numerical tolerance of zero).

IsSimplifyTrue.title: IsSimplifyTrue
IsSimplifyTrue.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether a mathematical expression simplifies to true (or within a numerical tolerance of zero).

IsSimplifyEqual.title: IsSimplifyEqual
IsSimplifyEqual.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether two expressions simplify to the same result (or are within a numerical tolerance of the same result).
syntax: Syntax 3:  IsSimplifyEqual( mathA, mathB,  absEpsilon, relEpsilon )

Strcmp.title: Strcmp
Strcmp.purpose: Purpose:  Compares two strings and returns: -1  if stringA    stringB

Kane.title: GetStaticsKane
Kane.purpose: Purpose:  Gets Kane's equations for statics or dynamics .

Lagrange.title: GetStaticsLagrange
Lagrange.purpose: Purpose:  Gets Lagrange's equations for statics or dynamics.
syntax: Syntax 3*: System.GetStaticsLagrange()  ** RESERVED SYNTAX, IN DEVELOPMENT.

Newton.title: GetStatics
Newton.purpose: Purpose:  Gets Newton/Euler statics or dynamics equations of motion.

List.title: List
List.purpose: Purpose:  Displays workspace contents on the screen.

log.title: log or log10
log.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the natural (base e) logarithm of a scalar expression. Returns the base 10 logarithm of a scalar expression.

Matrix.title: Matrix
Matrix.purpose: Purpose:  Constructs a matrix from a vector, dyadic, ...
syntax: Syntax 1:  Matrix( A,  vector>    )  or  Vector( A,  vector>  )
Syntax 2:  Matrix( A,  dyadic>>   )  or  Dyadic( A,  dyadic>> )

SkewMatrix.title: SkewMatrix
SkewMatrix.purpose: Purpose:  Constructs a skew-symmetric matrix from a vector.

min.title: min
min.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the smaller or larger of two real numbers.

NewtonianFrame.title: NewtonianFrame
NewtonianFrame.purpose: Purpose:  Declares a Newtonian (inertial) reference frame, which is a special RigidFrame in which  F = m*a  is valid.
syntax: Syntax 1:  NewtonianFrame N      (creates a Newtonian reference frame N)
Syntax 2:  NewtonianFrame        (displays the name of the NewtonianFrame)

numerator.title: numerator
numerator.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the numerator or a denominator of a fraction.

ODE.title: ODE
ODE.purpose: Purpose:  Executes or writes a MATLAB, C, or Fortran program to solve ordinary differential or coupled differential/algebraic equations.
syntax: Syntax 2:  ODE( EqnsToSolve, x1, x2, ... )   Filename.Ext
Syntax 3:  ODE()                             Filename.Ext( Tx, Ty, Tz )
Syntax 4:  ODE() [A = Astart, Astop, Astep]  Filename.Ext

Optimize.title: Optimize
Optimize.purpose: Purpose:  Forms a set of equations which when solved optimizes a function (e.g., finds a local minimum or maximum), with or without equality constraints.
syntax: Syntax:    eqnsZero = Optimize( CostFunction,  Constraints = 0,  x1, x2, ... )

SolveOptimize.title: SolveOptimize (RESERVED KEYWORD & SYNTAXES. IN-DEVELOPMENT)
SolveOptimize.purpose: Purpose:  Forms a set of equations which when solved optimizes a function (e.g., finds a local minimum or maximum), with or without equality constraints (e.g., a combination of statics, dynamics, and/or geometry and motion constraints).
syntax: Syntax 1:  SolveOptimize( CostFunction,  Constraints,  x1,  x2, ... )
Syntax 2:  SolveOptimize( CostFunction,  Constraints,  x1=Guess1,  x2=Guess2 )
Syntax 3:  answer = SolveOptimize( CostFunction, Constraints, x1=Guess1, x2=Guess2 )

Output.title: Output
Output.purpose: Purpose:  Causes programs created by the ODE and CODE command to create
syntax: Syntax 1:  Output       x1 optionalUnits1, x2 optionalUnits2, ...
Syntax 2:  ClearOutput()       or ClearOutput(     startInteger : stopInteger)
Syntax 3:  Output  or  OutputPlot  or  OutputEncode

Particle.title: Particle
syntax: Syntax 1:  Particle Q1, Q2, ...          (creates particles Q1, Q2, ...)
Syntax 2:  Particle                      (displays names of all Particles)

PathToMotionGenesis.title: PathToMotionGenesis
PathToMotionGenesis.purpose: Purpose:  Specifies the operating system path to the MotionGenesis folder. This is the top-level directory (folder) for the program. Its sub-directories contains files used by the program.

Pause.title: Pause
Pause.purpose: Purpose:  Suspends execution when running an input file.

pi.title: pi
pi.purpose: Purpose:  Name assigned to the following ratio associated with a circle: pi = CircumferenceOfCircle / DiameterOfCircle.

Point.title: Point
Point.purpose: Purpose:  Declares points.  Welds a point to a RigidFrame or RigidBody.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Point P1, P2, ...   (creates points P1, P2, ...)
Syntax 2:  Point P()           (creates point P, not welded to an object)
Syntax 3:  Point Q( B )        (creates point Q welded to B)
Syntax 4:  Point               (displays names of all Points)

RandomInteger.title: RandomInteger
RandomInteger.purpose: Purpose:  Returns a random positive integer.

ReplaceString.title: ReplaceString
ReplaceString.purpose: Purpose:  Replaces strings in an expression with new strings. Truncates numbers in an expression (possibly simplifying it) by using the number of significant digits determined by SetDigits.

RigidBody.title: RigidBody
syntax: Syntax 1:  RigidBody  A, B, ...     (creates rigid bodies A, B, ...)
Syntax 2:  RigidBody                (displays all names declared as RigidBody)

RigidFrame.title: RigidFrame
RigidFrame.purpose: Purpose:  Declares a rigid frame (reference frame). Welds a rigid frame to a RigidBody or another RigidFrame.
syntax: Syntax 1:  RigidFrame  A, B, ...    (creates rigid frames A, B, ...)
Syntax 2:  RigidFrame  B( D )       (creates rigid frame B welded to D)
Syntax 3:  RigidFrame               (displays names of all RigidFrames)

Round.title: Round
Round.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the integer nearest to a real number.

RoundDigits.title: RoundDigits
RoundDigits.purpose: Purpose:  Truncates numbers in an expression to a number of significant digits.

Rotate.title: Rotate
Rotate.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the rotation matrix (direction cosine matrix) associated with the right-handed rotation of a rigid basis (e.g., B) in another rigid basis (e.g., A).  Tries to form B's angular velocity/acceleration in A and in the NewtonianFrame.
syntax: Syntax 3:  B.Rotate( A, SequenceIJK, q1, q2, q3 )
Syntax 4:  B.Rotate( A, EulerParameters, e0, e1, e2, e3 )
Syntax 5:  B.Rotate( A, RodriguesParameters, rho1, rho2, rho3 )
Syntax 6:  B.RotateX( A, theta )     B.RotateNegativeX( A, theta )

RotateX.title: RotateX
RotateX.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the rotation matrix (direction cosine matrix) associated with the right-handed rotation of a rigid basis (e.g., B) in another rigid basis (e.g., A).  Tries to form B's angular velocity/acceleration in A and in the NewtonianFrame.

RotateY.title: RotateY
RotateY.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the rotation matrix (direction cosine matrix) associated with the right-handed rotation of a rigid basis (e.g., B) in another rigid basis (e.g., A).  Tries to form B's angular velocity/acceleration in A and in the NewtonianFrame.

RotateZ.title: RotateZ
RotateZ.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the rotation matrix (direction cosine matrix) associated with the right-handed rotation of a rigid basis (e.g., B) in another rigid basis (e.g., A).  Tries to form B's angular velocity/acceleration in A and in the NewtonianFrame.

Run.title: Run
Run.purpose: Purpose:  Reads (loads) a file containing a sequence of program commands.

Save.title: Save
Save.purpose: Purpose:  Creates a text file containing input or input and output. To print the file, open it with a text editor and print.

SetScratchDirectory.title: SetScratchDirectory
SetScratchDirectory.purpose: Purpose:  Specifies the operating system path to the directory used by the program for scratch files.

SetWorkingDirectory.title: SetWorkingDirectory
SetWorkingDirectory.purpose: Purpose:  Specifies the operating system path to write files.

SetAcceleration.title: SetAcceleration
SetAcceleration.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a point's acceleration in a reference frame.
syntax: Syntax 3:  Q.SetAcceleration( N, fromPoint, BFrame, BQ )
Syntax 3 may also set the following (if not in workspace):

SetAngularAcceleration.title: SetAngularAcceleration
SetAngularAcceleration.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a reference frame's angular acceleration.

SetAngularVelocity.title: SetAngularVelocity
SetAngularVelocity.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a reference frame's angular velocity.
syntax: Syntax 3:  B.SetAngularVelocity( A, SequenceIJ,  q1, q2 )
Syntax 4:  B.SetAngularVelocity( A, SequenceIJK, q1, q2, q3 )
Syntax 5:  B.SetAngularVelocity( A, EulerParameters, e0, e1, e2, e3 )
Syntax 6:  B.SetAngularVelocity( A, RodriguesParameters, rho1, rho2, rho3 )

SetAngularVelocityAcceleration.title: SetAngularVelocityAcceleration
SetAngularVelocityAcceleration.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a reference frame's angular velocity and angular acceleration.

SetAutoDifferentiate.title: SetAutoDifferentiate
SetAutoDifferentiate.purpose: Purpose:  Controls whether or not ordinary derivatives of quantities declared as a Variable or Specified are automatically calculated. In other words, controls whether or not y', y'', y''', etc., are automatically calculated and assigned when y is assigned.

SetAutoEpsilon.title: SetAutoEpsilon
SetAutoEpsilon.purpose: Purpose:  In every expression, rounds numbers suitably close to an integer.

SetAutoExpress.title: SetAutoExpress
SetAutoExpress.purpose: Purpose:  For each rigid body, determines whether the body's angular velocity and angular acceleration in the NewtonianFrame are automatically expressed in terms of the body's unit vectors or in terms of unit vectors associated with the body's inertia dyadic.

SetAutoFactor.title: SetAutoFactor
SetAutoFactor.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether or not to automatically factor expressions.

SetAutoOverWrite.title: SetAutoOverWrite
SetAutoOverWrite.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether to overwrite automatically or only when the user has responded affirmatively to a prompt.

SetAutoRhs.title: SetAutoRhs
SetAutoRhs.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether or not to replace symbols appearing on  the  left-hand  sides of equations with the right- hand  sides  of  the  equations when these symbols are used in expressions.

SetAutoSimplify.title: SetAutoSimplify
SetAutoSimplify.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether or not to automatically simplify certain mathematical expressions.

SetAutoTaylorSeries.title: SetAutoLinearize
SetAutoTaylorSeries.purpose: Purpose:  Automatically expands all expressions in Taylor series.

SetAutoZee.title: SetAutoZee
SetAutoZee.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether or not Zs are to automatically created.

SetBasisSubscripts.title: SetBasisSubscripts
SetBasisSubscripts.purpose: Purpose:  Designates the last alphanumeric character in the name of right-handed orthogonal, unit vectors created when a RigidBody, RigidFrame, or NewtonianFrame is declared.

SetBeepSoundOnError.title: SetBeepSoundOnError
SetBeepSoundOnError.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether or not to issue an audible beep sound when a warning or error message is issued.

SetCharge.title: SetCharge
SetCharge.purpose: Purpose:  Sets and returns the charge on a point or Particle.

SetCMSymbol.title: SetCMSymbol
SetCMSymbol.purpose: Purpose:  Designates the symbol for the center of mass of bodies.

SetComplex.title: SetComplex
SetComplex.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether to treat scalars as real or complex.

SetDigits.title: SetDigits
SetDigits.purpose: Purpose:  Sets an upper limit on the number of digits displayed on the screen when numerical values are reported. Governs the format and number of digits displayed in output files created in response to the ODE and Solve commands.

SetDt.title: SetDt
SetDt.purpose: Purpose:  Assigns a scalar quantity and its ordinary derivatives.

SetGeneralizedSpeed.title: SetGeneralizedSpeed
SetGeneralizedSpeed.purpose: Purpose: Names generalized speeds for Kane's method. Names generalized coordinates for Lagrange and Kane methods and for calculating generalized forces associated with potential energy. Returns a column matrix of generalized speeds or coordinates.

SetNoZeeSymbol.title: SetNoZeeSymbol
SetNoZeeSymbol.purpose: Purpose:  Identifies scalar quantities that are to be excluded from Zs.

SetLineStepping.title: SetLineStepping
SetLineStepping.purpose: Purpose:  Runs an input file one line at a time.

SetLineWidth.title: SetLineWidth
SetLineWidth.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the line width for writing output to .all or .html files (in conjunction with the SAVE command) or in writing C, Fortran, MATLAB(R) codes (in conjunction with the CODE or ODE commands).

SetMass.title: SetMass
SetMass.purpose: Purpose:  Sets and returns the mass of a Particle or RigidBody.

SetMassInertia.title: SetMassInertia
SetMassInertia.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a RigidBody's mass and inertia about its center of mass. Returns the associated inertia dyadic.
syntax: Syntax 1:  B.SetMassInertia( mB, IBxx, IByy, IBzz )
Syntax 2:  B.SetMassInertia( mB, IBxx, IByy, IBzz, IBxy, IByz, IBzx )
Syntax 3:  B.SetMassInertia( mB, aboutPoint, A, I11, I22, I33 )
Syntax 4:  B.SetMassInertia( mB, aboutPoint, A, I11, I22, I33, I12, I23, I31 )

SetInertia.title: SetInertia
SetInertia.purpose: Purpose:  Sets and returns a RigidBody's inertia dyadic about a point.
syntax: Syntax 1:  B.SetInertia( aboutPoint, IBxx, IByy, IBzz )
Syntax 2:  B.SetInertia( aboutPoint, IBxx, IByy, IBzz, IBxy, IByz, IBzx )
Syntax 3:  B.SetInertia( aboutPoint, A, I11, I22, I33 )
Syntax 4:  B.SetInertia( aboutPoint, A, I11, I22, I33, I12, I23, I31 )

SetPauseOnWarning.title: SetPauseOnWarning
SetPauseOnWarning.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the duration of the pause that occurs when a warning is issued during execution of an input file.

SetRotationMatrix.title: SetRotationMatrix
SetRotationMatrix.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the rotation matrix relating two sets of bases vectors associated with right-handed rotations of B relative to A.
syntax: Syntax 3:  B.SetRotationMatrix( A, SequenceIJ,  q1, q2 )
Syntax 4:  B.SetRotationMatrix( A, SequenceIJK, q1, q2, q3 )
Syntax 5:  B.SetRotationMatrix( A, EulerParameters, e0, e1, e2, e3 )
Syntax 6:  B.SetRotationMatrix( A, RodriguesParameters, rho1, rho2, rho3 )

SetRotationMatrixAndPosition.title: SetRotationMatrixAndPosition
SetRotationMatrixAndPosition.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the rotation matrix and position vector associated with two rigid frames (or rigid bodies).

SetRigidTransform.title: SetRigidTransform
SetRigidTransform.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the rotation matrix and position vector associated with two rigid frames (or rigid bodies).

GetRigidTransform.title: GetRigidTransform34
GetRigidTransform.purpose: Purpose:  Gets a matrix that contains the rotation matrix and position vector matrix associated with two rigid frames (or rigid bodies).

SetRotationMatrixODE.title: SetRotationMatrixODE
SetRotationMatrixODE.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the rotation matrix relating two sets of bases vectors. Forms differential equations that relate the time-derivatives of orientation angles, Euler parameters, Rodrigues parameters, or rotation matrix elements (direction cosines) to angular velocity measures.
syntax: Syntax 1:  B.SetRotationMatrixODE( A, SequenceIJK,      q1, q2, q3 )
Syntax 2:  B.SetRotationMatrixODE( A, EulerParameters,  e0, e1, e2, e3 )
Syntax 3:  B.SetRotationMatrixODE( A, RodriguesParameters, rho1, rho2, rho3 )
Syntax 4:  B.SetRotationMatrixODE( A, PoissonParameters, Rxx,Rxy, ..., Rzz )

SetEulerParameterODE.title: SetEulerParameterODE
SetEulerParameterODE.purpose: Purpose:  Forms differential equations by relating the time-derivatives of Euler parameters (quaternion) to angular velocity measures.
syntax: Syntax:  B.SetEulerParameterODE( A, e0, e1, e2, e3 )

SetRodriguesParameterODE.title: SetRodriguesParameterODE
SetRodriguesParameterODE.purpose: Purpose:  Forms differential equations by relating the time-derivatives of Rodrigues parameters to angular velocity measures.
syntax: Syntax:  B.SetRodriguesParameterODE( A, r1, r2, r3 )

SetPoissonParameterODE.title: SetPoissonParameterODE
SetPoissonParameterODE.purpose: Purpose:  Forms differential equations by relating the time-derivatives of Rodrigues parameters to angular velocity measures.
syntax: Syntax:  B.SetPoissonParameterODE( A, Rxx,Rxy,Rxz, Ryx,Ryy,Ryz, Rzx,Rzy,Rzz )

SetRotationMatrixX.title: SetRotationMatrixX
SetRotationMatrixX.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the rotation matrix (direction cosine matrix) associated with the right-handed rotation of a rigid basis (e.g., B) relative to another rigid basis (e.g., A).

SetRotationMatrixY.title: SetRotationMatrixY
SetRotationMatrixY.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the rotation matrix (direction cosine matrix) associated with the right-handed rotation of a rigid basis (e.g., B) relative to another rigid basis (e.g., A).

SetRotationMatrixZ.title: SetRotationMatrixZ
SetRotationMatrixZ.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the rotation matrix (direction cosine matrix) associated with the right-handed rotation of a rigid basis (e.g., B) relative to another rigid basis (e.g., A).

SetPosition.title: SetPosition
SetPosition.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a point's position from another point.

SetPositionVelocity.title: SetPositionVelocity
SetPositionVelocity.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a point's position from another point. Sets a point's velocity in a reference frame.
syntax: Syntax 3:  Q.SetPositionVelocity( fromPoint, positionVector, BFrame, BQ )
Syntax 2:  Q.SetPositionVelocity( refFrame, fromPoint, positionVector, BFixed )

SetScreenDisplay.title: SetScreenDisplay
SetScreenDisplay.purpose: Purpose:  Determines whether or not to print input lines or response lines to the screen.

SetSFunctionInput.title: SetSFunctionInput
SetSFunctionInput.purpose: Purpose:  Identifies scalar quantities that are to be used by the ODE and Solve commands while generating .mSFunction and .cSFunction codes. These quantities are inputs to SFunction blocks.

SetPlotInterface.title: SetPlotInterface
SetPlotInterface.purpose: Purpose:  Designates how plots are displayed.

SetUnitSystem.title: SetUnitSystem
SetUnitSystem.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a unit system for unit conversions, e.g., for units set in Input and Output statements and Evaluate and Solve commands.

SetVelocity.title: SetVelocity
SetVelocity.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a point's velocity in a reference frame.
syntax: Syntax 3:  Q.SetVelocity( N, fromPoint, BFrame, BQ )

SetVelocityAcceleration.title: SetVelocityAcceleration
SetVelocityAcceleration.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a point's velocity and acceleration in a reference frame.
syntax: Syntax 3:  Q.SetVelocityAcceleration( N, fromPoint, BFrame, BQ )

SetWorkODE.title: SetWorkODE
SetWorkODE.purpose: Purpose:  Forms an ODE (ordinary differential equation) that governs the work of a force or torque (or set of forces and/or torques).

sign.title: sign
sign.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the sign of a real scalar expression.
syntax: Syntax 1:  sign( x )               % 1 if x > 0,   0 if x = 0,   -1 if x = 0,  otherwise -1.
Syntax 3:  sign0IsNegative1( x )   % Returns -1  if x ceil  floor  int  max  min  round  sign  MathFunctions

sin.title: sin and csc
sin.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the sine or cosecant of a scalar expression.

Solve.title: Solve
Solve.purpose: Purpose:  Solves linear and nonlinear algebraic equations.
syntax: Syntax 3:  answer = Solve( METHOD, EqnsToSolve,  x1,  x2,  ... )
Syntax 4:  answer = Solve(         EqnsToSolve,  x1=Guess1,  x2=Guess2,  ... )

SolveDt.title: SolveDt
SolveDt.purpose: Purpose:  Solves _linear_ algebraic equations. Differentiates the equations and solves for derivatives.

SolveSetInput.title: SolveSetInput
SolveSetInput.purpose: Purpose:  Solves NUMERICAL linear and nonlinear algebraic equations and sets Input values for the unknowns.

SolveSetInputDt.title: SolveSetInputDt
SolveSetInputDt.purpose: Purpose:  Solves linear and nonlinear algebraic equations to set NUMERICAL

SolveQuadraticPositiveRoot.title: SolveQuadraticPositiveRoot,
SolveQuadraticPositiveRoot.purpose: Purpose:  Solve for the positive or negative roots of a polynomial of degree 2 (and possibly its ordinary derivatives).

Specified.title: Specified
Specified.purpose: Purpose:  Declares the names of specified quantities. Creates an array of all names declared as Specified.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Specified        a,  b,  x+,  y-,  ...

Spline.title: Spline
Spline.purpose: Purpose:  Fits a curve through two points with various functions: transition, step, pulse, bell, and polynomial.  The examples below use a function y(t) that connects points (t0,y0) and (tF,yF).
syntax: Syntax 1:  Spline( TRANSITION, t, t0, tF, y0, yF )
Syntax 2:  Spline( STEP,       t, t0, tF, y0, yF )
Syntax 3:  Spline( PULSE,      t, t0, tF, y0, yExtremum )
Syntax 4:  Spline( BELL,       t, t0, tF, y0, yExtremum )
Syntax 5:  Spline( LINE,       t, t0, tF, y0, yF )
Syntax 6:  Spline( CUBIC,      t, t0, tF, y0, yF, dy/dt(t0), dy/dt(tF) )

Squared.title: Squared
Squared.purpose: Purpose:  Forms the square of a scalar, a matrix, or a vector.

sqrt.title: sqrt
sqrt.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the square-root of a scalar expression.

System.title: System
System.purpose: Purpose:  Defines a collection of the following physical objects: Point,  Particle,  RigidFrame,  RigidBody.
syntax: Syntax 1:  System  Fred( A, B, C )

t.title: t
t.purpose: Purpose:  Default name assigned to the independent variable. The program initiates with a declaration of the form: IndependentVariable  t

tan.title: tan and cot
tan.purpose: Purpose:  Returns the tangent or cotangent of a scalar expression.

Translate.title: Translate
Translate.purpose: Purpose:  Sets a point's position from another point. Sets a point's velocity in a reference frame. Sets a point's acceleration in a reference frame.
syntax: Syntax 3:  Q.Translate( fromPoint, positionVector, BFrame, BQ )
Syntax 2:  Q.Translate( refFrame, fromPoint, positionVector, BFixed )
Syntax 3:  Q.Translate( refFrame, fromPoint, positionVector, BFrame, BQ )

TrueFalse.title: true
TrueFalse.purpose: Purpose:  Special names that are helpful for true/false logic.
syntax: Syntax 1: true        % true  simplifies to 1 in math expressions.
Syntax 2: false       % false simplifies to 0 in math expressions.

SetVelocityAccelerationForGetGeometry.title: SetVelocityAccelerationForGetGeometry
SetVelocityAccelerationForGetGeometry.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the velocity and acceleration in the NewtonianFrame of the origin and center of mass of a rigid body.

Variable.title: Variable
Variable.purpose: Purpose:  Declares the names of variables.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Variable  a,  b,  x+,  y-,  ...
Syntax 3:  Variable  (displays all names declared as Variable)

Vector.title: Vector
Vector.purpose: Purpose:  Constructs a vector or three-element column matrix.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Vector( A,  x, y, z  )

Zee.title: Zee
Zee.purpose: Purpose:  Introduces  Z1, Z2, ...  as  names for expressions and makes corresponding replacements.

----------------------------------------.title: The remaining functions below are or will be superseded by other methods.

Algebraic.title: Algebraic
Algebraic.purpose: Purpose:  Writes a MATLAB, C, or Fortran program to solve for Output and perhaps to solve coupled LINEAR algebraic equations.
syntax: Syntax 2:  Algebraic( EqnsToSolve, x1, x2, ... )   Filename.Ext
Syntax 3:  Algebraic()                             Filename.Ext( Tx, Ty, Tz )
Syntax 4:  Algebraic() [A = Astart, Astop, Astep]  Filename.Ext

Angvel.title: Angvel
Angvel.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates and sets angular velocity in terms of orientation angles, Euler parameters, Rodrigues parameters, or elements of rotation matrices (direction cosines) and their time-derivatives.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Angvel(      A, B, TYPE,  q1, q2, q3 )
Syntax 2:  Angvel(      A, B, Euler, e1, e2, e3, e4 )
Syntax 3:  Angvel(      A, B, Rodrigues, rho1, rho2, rho3 )

Code.title: CODE
Code.purpose: Purpose:  Writes a Fortran, C, or MATLAB program or immediately executes a program for a variety of purposes.
syntax: Syntax 3:  Code Nonlinear( MatrixOfEqns, UNKNOWNS ) Filename.Ext

Nonlinear.title: Nonlinear
Nonlinear.purpose: Purpose:  Writes a MATLAB, C, or Fortran program to solve coupled nonlinear algebraic equations and calculate Output.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Nonlinear( EqnsToSolve, x1, x2, ... )  Filename.Ext
Syntax 2:  Nonlinear( EqnsToSolve, x1, x2, ... )  Filename.Ext( Tx, Ty, Tz )
Syntax 3:  Nonlinear( EqnsToSolve, x1, x2, ... )  [A = Astart, Astop, Astep]

Dircos.title: Dircos
Dircos.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates and sets the direction cosine matrix (rotation matrix) relating two sets of dextral, orthogonal unit vectors in terms of orientation angles, Euler parameters, or Rodrigues parameters.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Dircos( A, B, TYPE,  q1, q2, q3 )
Syntax 2:  Dircos( A, B, EULER, e1, e2, e3, e4)
Syntax 3:  Dircos( A, B, RODRIGUES, rho1, rho2, rho3 )

FrStar.title: FrStar
FrStar.purpose: Purpose:  Forms  expressions  for  generalized inertia forces or contributions to generalized inertia forces.

Gyrostat.title: Gyrostat
Gyrostat.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates expressions related to angular momentum, kinetic energy, and generalized inertia forces for systems containing gyrostats.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Gyrostat( Quantity, CYLINDER, Carrier, Rotor, J )
Syntax 2:  Gyrostat( Quantity, SPHERE,   Carrier, Rotor, K )

KinDiffs.title: KinDiffs
KinDiffs.purpose: Purpose:  Sets the first time-derivatives of orientation angles, Euler parameters, Rodrigues parameters, or direction cosines in terms of angular velocity measure numbers.
syntax: Syntax 1:  KinDiffs( A, B, SequenceIJK,  q1, q2, q3 )
Syntax 2:  KinDiffs( A, B, Euler, e1, e2, e3, e4 )
Syntax 3:  KinDiffs( A, B, Rodrigues, rho1, rho2, rho3 )
Syntax 4:  KinDiffs( A, B  Poisson, C11,C12,C13,

Simprot.title: Simprot
Simprot.purpose: Purpose:  Forms rotation matrices associated with a simple rotation of one reference frame in another reference frame.
syntax: Syntax 1:  Simprot( A, B, Integer, Angle )
Syntax 2:  Simprot( A, B, Vec,     Angle )

V1PT.title: V1PT
V1PT.purpose: Purpose:  Calculates and sets the velocity (or acceleration) of a point moving on a reference frame.
syntax: Syntax 1:  V1PT( N, B, BQ, Q )
Syntax 2:  A1PT( N, B, BQ, Q )

V2PTS.title: V2PTS
V2PTS.purpose: Purpose:  For two points fixed on a reference frame (RigidBody or RigidFrame) calculates and sets the velocity (or acceleration) of one point from the velocity (or acceleration) of the other.
syntax: Syntax 1:  V2PTS(  N, B, P, Q )
Syntax 2:  A2PTS(  N, B, P, Q )
Syntax 3:  VA2PTS( N, B, P, Q )
